topic.title: Other maintenance workflows
topic.para: TODO Here's a list of other maintenance workflows if this one wasn't for you

link.citetitle: Performing maintenance tasks on cluster infrastructure

listitem.para: This article describes how to perform cluster-wide maintenance tasks that don't require rebooting the nodes. In this scenario, most resources don't need any downtime. However, &dlm; and &dlm;-dependent resources must be stopped.

link.citetitle: Performing maintenance tasks on cluster nodes

listitem.para: This article describes how to perform maintenance tasks that require rebooting the nodes. In this scenario, the resources usually don't require downtime as they are moved off to other nodes. However, if no other node is eligible to run a resource, that resource stops instead. But like... what am I going to do about stopping the cluster but not rebooting?

link.citetitle: Performing maintenance tasks on multiple resources

listitem.para: This article describes how to perform maintenance tasks on multiple resources, either across the whole cluster or all on one node. In this scenario, the resources are placed in maintenance mode so that you can manage them manually without any interference from the cluster.

link.citetitle: Performing maintenance tasks on a single resource

listitem.para: This article describes how to perform maintenance tasks on just one resource. In this scenario, the resource is placed in maintenance mode so that you can manage it manually without any interference from the cluster. Alternatively, you can place the resource in unmanaged mode, which is similar to maintenance mode but the cluster continues to monitor the resource and report failures.
